reasoning: The 2025 National Heads-Up Poker Championship was a 64-player invitational that took place in August 2025, functioning as a pre-recorded event to be broadcast later. John Smith was one of the 64 participants. In mid-October 2025, extensive reporting from poker and gaming outlets revealed that the results had been leaked on Polymarket. Specifically, the leaked data points overwhelmingly to Sam Soverel as the winner, with Soverel's odds on the prediction market peaking at over 99% and consistently trading above 90%. Given that Soverel is heavily confirmed as the winner by insider leaks, and John Smith's base rate as simply one of the other 63 competitors was only 1.5% to begin with, the chance that Smith actually won is essentially negligible. Therefore, I am assigning the lowest valid probability strictly permitted by the tool bounds (0.02).
